options.text: Converting an image from one style or domain into another.
Moving an image file from one folder to another.
Translating the text labels inside an image file.

exercises.explain: Image-to-image translation acts like an AI painter, taking an image in one format (like a black-and-white sketch) and translating it into another (like a colorful photo).
exercises.prompt: What is the main goal of Image-to-Image translation in AI?

cards.body: Ever wished you could turn your rough napkin sketches into realistic, professional photos with a single click? Image-to-image translation makes this magic possible!
cards.title: Sketch to Reality
What is Image Translation?
cards.body: Image-to-Image translation is the process of mapping an input image from one domain (like a simple outline) to a corresponding output image in a different domain (like a photorealistic rendering) while keeping the same structure.

cards.title: Like Learning a Language
cards.body: Think of it like language translation. Instead of converting English words into Spanish words while keeping the same meaning, you are converting "sketch pixels" into "photo pixels" while keeping the same shapes.
A classic example is Pix2Pix. You give the AI a black-and-white blueprint of a house, and it instantly generates a fully textured, colored image of what the building would look like in real life.
cards.title: Blueprints to Buildings
Not Just Coloring In
cards.body: Myth: The AI just colors inside the lines like a digital coloring book. Reality: The AI actually generates entirely new, realistic textures, lighting, shadows, and details based on what it learned from real photos!

right.text: Realistic leather handbag with metal zippers

left.text: Black and white edge sketch of a handbag

cards.body: See how the AI transforms structural outlines directly into realistic visual domains.
cards.title: Domain Mapping

exercises.explain: Image-to-image translation preserves the structure and layout of the original image while modifying its visual style or domain.
exercises.prompt: How does an Image-to-Image translation model work?

options.text: It completely rearranges the objects to create a new layout.
It translates pixels from a source domain into a target domain while preserving structural layout.
It deletes the background of the image to make it transparent.

exercises.explain: Yes! Image-to-image translation can work both ways, turning sketches into photos, or photos back into sketches.
exercises.prompt: True or False: Image translation models can be trained to turn photos back into simple sketches.

exercises.sentence: Image-to-image translation maps an image from a source [blank] to a target one.
exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank: Image-to-image translation maps an image from a source _____ to a target one.
exercises.explain: Image translation models map images from a source domain (like sketches) to a target domain (like realistic photos).

exercises.prompt: Which of these is an example of generative Image-to-Image Translation?
exercises.explain: Generating a realistic street view from a simple semantic map of colored blocks is a classic case of image-to-image translation. cropping is just a standard utility.

options.text: Turning a colored label map of blocks into a realistic street scene.
Cropping a photo of a street down to a square size.
Training images that consist of exact matching pairs from both domains.
Images that only contain text descriptions.

exercises.explain: Paired data consists of exact matching pairs (e.g., a sketch and its corresponding exact photo). This helps the AI learn the direct mapping. Up next, we will explore Pix2Pix!
exercises.prompt: In supervised image translation, what is 'paired data'?

title: Image-to-Image Translation
concept: Image translation maps an input image from one domain (e.g. edge sketch) to an output image in another domain (e.g. realistic photo).
